original_title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
selection_reasoning: This represents a fundamental shift in attack methodology - targeting the creators rather than just their creations. The concept of corrupting the forge-masters to taint all future works provides rich narrative material about trust, the sanctity of creation spaces, and how modern threats evolve beyond traditional boundaries.

field_note.title: The Corrupted Forges
field_note.subtitle: When shadows target the smiths instead of their steel
field_note.excerpt: A new darkness spreads not by tainting finished works, but by corrupting the very forges where trusted tools are made.
field_note.epigraph: The greatest deception is not to corrupt the creation, but the creator.
body_paragraphs: In ages past, those who sought to taint the great supply routes would intercept caravans or corrupt waystation stores. Their goal was simple: slip poisoned goods into trusted shipments and let distribution do their work. But the shadowed ones have learned a deeper art - why corrupt the goods when you can corrupt the forges themselves?

Three coordinated strikes within a single turning of days revealed this evolved strategy. Attackers infiltrated the great repositories where artificers store their tools and components, but they sought neither to plant false artifacts nor to steal the works themselves. Instead, they hunted for the keys, seals, and sacred writs that grant access to the forges - the API tokens, cloud credentials, and authentication secrets that modern smiths use to craft their creations.

With these stolen authorities, the corruption can flow backward through the very channels meant to ensure purity. A compromised forge-key allows attackers to push tainted components upstream, to inject malicious enchantments at the moment of creation rather than after. Every tool made in such corrupted forges carries the seed of betrayal, trusted because it bears the maker's authentic seal.

This represents a fundamental shift in how we must guard our realms. No longer can we focus solely on inspecting finished goods at the borders. The workshops, the personal sanctums of our creators, the very tools they use to shape tomorrow's defenses - all have become part of the battlefield. The age of assuming the forge itself is sacred has ended.
